# datahub_policy (Resource)

**DataHub ✅ | DataHub Cloud ✅**

Creates and manages a DataHub access policy (`dataHubPolicy`).

Policies grant a set of privileges to a set of actors (users and/or groups), optionally scoped to a set of resources. There are two policy types:

- `PLATFORM` -- top-level administrative privileges (e.g. `MANAGE_POLICIES`, `MANAGE_INGESTION`). Omit the `resources` block.
- `METADATA` -- privileges over metadata entities (e.g. `EDIT_ENTITY_TAGS`), optionally scoped via the `resources` block.

## Naming

`policy_id` becomes the URN suffix (`urn:li:dataHubPolicy:<policy_id>`). Supplying an explicit id keeps the URN deterministic and stable, avoiding the random UUID the DataHub UI assigns.

## List ownership

This resource owns the complete `privileges`, `actors`, and `resources` sets and writes the full desired state on every apply. Privileges or actors added outside Terraform are removed on the next apply. These are modeled as sets, so element order is not significant.

## Privileges

`privileges` are free-form strings and are not validated by the provider, since the valid set differs between DataHub releases and between OSS and DataHub Cloud. See the DataHub `PoliciesConfig` for the authoritative list.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "datahub_corp_group" "data_platform" {
  group_id = "data-platform"
  name     = "Data Platform Team"
}

# Platform policy: grant administrative privileges to a group.
resource "datahub_policy" "platform_admins" {
  policy_id   = "data-platform-admins"
  name        = "Data Platform Admins"
  type        = "PLATFORM"
  description = "Lets the data platform team manage ingestion and secrets"
  privileges  = ["MANAGE_INGESTION", "MANAGE_SECRETS"]

  actors = {
    groups = [datahub_corp_group.data_platform.urn]
  }
}

# Metadata policy: scope privileges to specific resources.
resource "datahub_policy" "tag_editors" {
  policy_id  = "prod-dataset-tag-editors"
  name       = "Prod Dataset Tag Editors"
  type       = "METADATA"
  privileges = ["EDIT_ENTITY_TAGS"]

  actors = {
    groups = [datahub_corp_group.data_platform.urn]
  }

  resources = {
    type      = "dataset"
    resources = ["urn:li:dataset:(urn:li:dataPlatform:hive,sales.transactions,PROD)"]
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `actors` (Attributes) The actors the policy's privileges are granted to. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--actors))
- `name` (String) Human-readable display name for the policy.
- `policy_id` (String) Unique identifier for the policy. Becomes the URN suffix (`urn:li:dataHubPolicy:<policy_id>`). Changing this forces a new resource.
- `privileges` (Set of String) Set of privilege strings the policy grants (e.g. `["MANAGE_POLICIES"]`). Not validated by the provider.
- `type` (String) Policy type: `PLATFORM` or `METADATA`.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the policy's purpose.
- `resources` (Attributes) Resource scope for METADATA policies. Omit for platform-wide PLATFORM policies. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--resources))
- `state` (String) Policy state: `ACTIVE` (enforced) or `INACTIVE`. Defaults to `ACTIVE`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `urn` (String) Full DataHub URN for this policy (e.g., `urn:li:dataHubPolicy:my-policy`).

<a id="nestedatt--actors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `actors`

Optional:

- `all_groups` (Boolean) Apply the policy to all groups. Defaults to `false`.
- `all_users` (Boolean) Apply the policy to all users. Defaults to `false`.
- `groups` (Set of String) Set of group URNs (e.g. `["urn:li:corpGroup:data-platform"]`).
- `resource_owners` (Boolean) Apply the policy to owners of the targeted resource (METADATA policies only). Defaults to `false`.
- `resource_owners_types` (Set of String) Set of ownership-type URNs the resource_owners filter applies to (when `resource_owners = true`).
- `users` (Set of String) Set of user URNs (e.g. `["urn:li:corpuser:alice"]`).


<a id="nestedatt--resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `resources`

Optional:

- `all_resources` (Boolean) Apply to all resources of `type`. Defaults to `false`.
- `resources` (Set of String) Set of specific resource URNs the policy applies to.
- `type` (String) The resource type the policy applies to (e.g. `dataset`).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by bare policy ID (provider constructs the URN)
terraform import datahub_policy.platform_admins data-platform-admins

# Or import by full URN
terraform import datahub_policy.platform_admins urn:li:dataHubPolicy:data-platform-admins
```
